elements.text: (ii) Changes to PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all PAR findings, including those submitted through the PCS review process or SRC
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, if a Seller or, if applicable, a Related Third Party, becomes aware of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: information that could impact, or that is inconsistent with, a project’s PAR findings, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller or Related Third Party must contact Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: ProjectInfo@FreddieMac.com and provide the specific information. Seller must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as soon as feasible but no later than five business days after Seller becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: aware of such information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Condominium Project’s PAR findings (status and/or feedback messages) may change
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on information assessed after CPA assigns a status to the project and after Seller last
elements.label: text

elements.text: accessed CPA and obtained a project’s PAR findings.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: impact, or that is inconsistent with, a project’s PAR findings, then, prior to sale of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit Mortgage secured by a unit in that Condominium Project, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm the Condominium Project’s current PAR findings; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure compliance with the requirement(s) specified in the last PAR findings (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.10(d)(iii) below)

elements.text: (iii)Last PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: findings if the date listed in the “Date Issued” field on the Condo Project Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Feedback Certificate is later than the date listed in the “Date/Time Assessed” field in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transaction Details section of the Last Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Otherwise, the PAR findings on the Last Feedback Certificate are the last PAR findings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If submissions in CPA and Loan Product Advisor are made on the same day, then the PAR
elements.label: text

elements.text: findings on the Last Feedback Certificate are the last PAR findings.

elements.text: (f) Project Certified Submission (PCS) review process
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA may specify on certain PARs, or the Loan Product Advisor Feedback Certificate may
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicate, that a Condominium Project is eligible to be submitted for a Project Certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: review through the CPA PCS process. Such submissions may be made only through CPA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to submission, the Seller is not required to determine that a Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of Sections 5701.3 (Ineligible projects), 5701.5 (Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects), and the Glossary definition of an Established Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If CPA finds that the Condominium Project meets the requirements for Project Certified, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project will be assigned a Project Certified status. The provisions of Section 5701.10
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable to projects with a Project Certified status are also applicable to projects with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project Certified status obtained through a PCS review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) PCS timing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project must be submitted and receive a Project Certified status PAR finding prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale of the related Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) PCS requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must submit the following documents to Freddie Mac for review:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller full review questionnaire dated within 180 days of PCS request date;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Project Addendum Form 476A or similar documentation to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: if a project is in need of Critical Repairs;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any structural and/or mechanical inspection that has been completed within 3 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Seller’s PCS request date;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Current approved budget; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such other documentation/information as Freddie Mac may request from time to time
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for the information required by the PCS review, Sellers must not provide any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-related information or other Mortgage information in connection with their
elements.label: text

elements.text: PCS request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Seller Review Certification (SRC) process
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA may specify on certain PARs, or the Loan Product Advisor Feedback Certificate may
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicate, that a Condominium Project is eligible to be submitted for a Seller Certified review
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the CPA SRC process. Such submissions may be made only through CPA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Seller chooses to submit an SRC in CPA, Seller will be prompted to respond to a series of
elements.label: text

elements.text: questions based on the Condominium Project’s PAR finding of Green, Yellow or Incomplete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assessment status (the “Pre-SRC PAR status”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a project receives a Seller Certified status PAR finding:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status will be visible only to CPA and Loan Product Advisor users who have the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same Seller ID as the SRC submitter; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status will be detailed on all Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificates and Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor Feedback Certificates for Mortgages secured by Condominium Units in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the same Condominium Project until the Seller Certified status expires
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller Certified status PAR finding may change prior to its expiration date if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status changes to Not Eligible or Project Certified, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is an SRC resubmission by a user with the same Seller ID as the SRC submitter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Documentation requirements for Mortgages with PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all PAR findings, the Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate or the Last Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate, whichever contains the last PAR findings as defined in Section 5701.10(d)(iii),
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintained in the Mortgage file; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Dated within 120 days of the Note Date or has an unexpired Seller Certified status (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.10(b)(ii) above for Seller Certified status expiration)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may upload in CPA all documentation related to its SRC at the time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission. The Seller must retain, and provide upon request, documentation to support its
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis that the Condominium Project complies with the project review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed by Seller as required in Section 5701.2(b)(6).

elements.text: does not assess; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All other requirements of the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Incomplete Assessment status PAR finding
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an Incomplete Assessment status PAR finding, Seller must ensure compliance with:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All project review requirements (Section 5701.2(a)); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All general eligibility requirements (Section 5701.2(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: All eligible Mortgages with PAR findings must be delivered to Freddie Mac within 120 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Note Date. If the Mortgage is not delivered within 120 days after the Note Date, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must submit a new PAR and obtain a new Condo Project Advisor Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate, or, if applicable, the Mortgage may be resubmitted to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to Section 5101.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the requirements for Condominium Projects with a Project Certified status in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.10 are met, then Seller is not required to comply with the requirements for any of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the project review types in Sections 5701.4 (Streamlined reviews), 5701.5 (Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project reviews), 5701.6 (New Condominium Project reviews), 5701.7 (projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: exempt from review) and 5701.9 (Reciprocal project reviews).

elements.text: 5701.10: Condo Project Advisor® – Project
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Assessment Request (PAR) (Future effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective for Mortgages with Application Received Dates on or after August 3, 2026.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condo Project Advisor® (CPASM) allows authorized Sellers to submit a Project Assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Request (PAR) to obtain feedback (referred to as the PAR findings) regarding a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Project’s PAR findings are also available in Loan Product Advisor® when CPA identifies the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property address submitted in Loan Product Advisor and the project has a PAR status assigned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgages eligible for purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for purchase the Mortgage must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be secured by a Condominium Unit in an Established Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a Loan Product Advisor Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be secured by a Condominium Unit in a Condominium Project with a Not Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: status PAR finding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Submission timing and Seller Certified status expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Submission timing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CPA, the PAR and, if applicable, the required data for a Seller Review Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (SRC), may be submitted at any time during the loan origination process but must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted prior to sale of the related Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor, the Mortgage must be submitted to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the requirements in Section 5101.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller Certified status expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller Certified status PAR finding expires one year from the SRC submission date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The expiration date is specified on the Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Last Feedback Certificate, whichever contains the last PAR findings as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.10(d)(iii). If the Seller Certified status PAR finding expires before the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Seller wants to rely on the Seller Certified status, Sellers must submit a new SRC in
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA prior to sale of the related Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The PAR is applicable only to the Mortgage identified on the Condo Project Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Feedback Certificate or the Last Feedback Certificate, as applicable, and is not applicable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other Mortgage secured by a Condominium Unit located in the same Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage identified will be the Mortgage for which the Seller enters one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following in CPA:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The LP Key Number, an alphanumeric identifier, listed on the Feedback Certificate,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which may also be referred to as the LP AUS Key Number or the Loan Prospector Key
elements.label: text

elements.text: Number, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller loan number/identifier
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Retail Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Retail Mortgages, the LP Key Number or the Seller loan number must be entered into
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA, and it must be identical to the LP Key Number or Seller loan number delivered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Non-Retail Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For non-Retail Mortgages, the LP Key Number must be entered into CPA, and it must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: identical to the LP Key Number delivered in Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller is responsible for ensuring compliance with the project review requirements specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the last PAR findings (see Section 5701.10(d)(iii) below). See Section 5701.10(i) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: details about representations and warranties.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project assigned a “Not Eligible” status by CPA. See table below.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as soon as feasible but no later than five business days after Seller becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: aware of such information.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: accessed CPA and obtained a project’s PAR findings.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure compliance with the requirement(s) specified in the last PAR findings (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.10(d)(iii) below)

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Transaction Details section of the Last Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Otherwise, the PAR findings on the Last Feedback Certificate are the last PAR findings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If submissions in CPA and Loan Product Advisor are made on the same day, then the PAR
elements.label: text

elements.text: findings on the Last Feedback Certificate are the last PAR findings.

elements.text: (f) Project Certified Submission (PCS) review process
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA may specify on certain PARs, or the Loan Product Advisor Feedback Certificate may
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicate, that a Condominium Project is eligible to be submitted for a Project Certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: review through the CPA PCS process. Such submissions may be made only through CPA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to submission, the Seller is not required to determine that a Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of Sections 5701.3 (Ineligible projects), 5701.5 (Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects), and the Glossary definition of an Established Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If CPA finds that the Condominium Project meets the requirements for Project Certified, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project will be assigned a Project Certified status. The provisions of Section 5701.10
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable to projects with a Project Certified status are also applicable to projects with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project Certified status obtained through a PCS review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) PCS timing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project must be submitted and receive a Project Certified status PAR finding prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale of the related Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) PCS requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must submit the following documents to Freddie Mac for review:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller full review questionnaire dated within 180 days of PCS request date;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Project Addendum Form 476A or similar documentation to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: if a project is in need of Critical Repairs;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any structural and/or mechanical inspection that has been completed within 3 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Seller’s PCS request date;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Current approved budget; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such other documentation/information as Freddie Mac may request from time to time
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for the information required by the PCS review, Sellers must not provide any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower-related information or other Mortgage information in connection with their
elements.label: text

elements.text: PCS request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Seller Review Certification (SRC) process
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA may specify on certain PARs, or the Loan Product Advisor Feedback Certificate may
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicate, that a Condominium Project is eligible to be submitted for a Seller Certified review
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the CPA SRC process. Such submissions may be made only through CPA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Seller chooses to submit an SRC in CPA, Seller will be prompted to respond to a series of
elements.label: text

elements.text: questions based on the Condominium Project’s PAR finding of Green, Yellow or Incomplete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assessment status (the “Pre-SRC PAR status”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a project receives a Seller Certified status PAR finding:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status will be visible only to CPA and Loan Product Advisor users who have the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same Seller ID as the SRC submitter; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status will be detailed on all Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificates and Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Product Advisor Feedback Certificates for Mortgages secured by Condominium Units in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the same Condominium Project until the Seller Certified status expires
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller Certified status PAR finding may change prior to its expiration date if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status changes to Not Eligible or Project Certified, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There is an SRC resubmission by a user with the same Seller ID as the SRC submitter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Documentation requirements for Mortgages with PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all PAR findings, the Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate or the Last Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate, whichever contains the last PAR findings as defined in Section 5701.10(d)(iii),
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintained in the Mortgage file; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Dated within 120 days of the Note Date or has an unexpired Seller Certified status (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.10(b)(ii) above for Seller Certified status expiration)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may upload in CPA all documentation related to its SRC at the time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission. The Seller must retain, and provide upon request, documentation to support its
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis that the Condominium Project complies with the project review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed by Seller as required in Section 5701.2(b)(6).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Project Certified status PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a subject Mortgage secured by a Condominium Unit in a Condominium Project with
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Project Certified status PAR finding, Freddie Mac will not exercise its remedies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the issuance of repurchase requests, in connection with a breach of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling representations and warranties related to the project review requirements that CPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.11: Condo Project Advisor® – Project Waiver
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Request (PWR) (05/06/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condo Project Advisor® (CPASM) allows authorized Sellers to submit a request for project
elements.label: text

elements.text: waiver requests (referred to as a Project Waiver Request (PWR)) for Established Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects that do not meet certain Condominium Project eligibility requirements. Approved PWRs
elements.label: text

elements.text: are issued a Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation requirements for Mortgages with approved PWRs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Established Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for a PWR, the Condominium Unit Mortgage must be secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit in an Established Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, a Condominium Project that meets all other requirements for an Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project, other than the 75% percentage conveyance to unit purchasers,
elements.label: text

elements.text: may still be considered as an Established Condominium Project on a case-by-case basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The developer retained more than 25% of the units for rental purposes; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The developer has owned these units for a minimum of 10 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Project eligibility categories
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Condominium Project must comply with the project eligibility requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Established Condominium Projects detailed in Section 5701.5 and all other applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Chapter 5701, except for the following Condominium Project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: categories:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Delinquent assessments – Section 5701.5(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Excessive commercial space – Section 5701.3(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Project in which the unit owners do not possess sole ownership of the Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements (referred to as “Leased Amenities” in CPA) – Section 5701.3(h)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Pending litigation (includes Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) proceedings) –
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.3(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Reserves for capital expenditures and deferred maintenance – Section 5701.5(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Excessive single investor concentration (referred to as “Single Entity Ownership” in
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA) – Section 5701.3(j)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Submission timing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The PWR may be submitted at any time during the loan origination process but must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted prior to sale of the related Condominium Unit Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Mortgage identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: An approved PWR is applicable to only the Mortgage identified on the Condo Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor Feedback Certificate and is not applicable to any other Mortgage secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit located in the same Condominium Project. The Mortgage identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be the Mortgage in connection with which the Seller enters one of the following in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the “Enter ID/Key” field in CPA:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Loan Product Advisor key alphanumeric identifier
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Fannie Mae Desktop Underwriter® key alphanumeric identifier (referred to as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Casefile ID in Desktop Underwriter), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Doc File ID alphanumeric identifier issued in connection with submission of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal data to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal®
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documentation requirements for Mortgages with approved PWRs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate must be maintained in the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with a Mortgage with an approved PWR, Freddie Mac will accept the Condo
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project Advisor Feedback Certificate and will not exercise its remedies, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: issuance of repurchase requests, in connection with a breach of the Seller’s selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations and warranties related to the Condominium Project review and eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements identified on the Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller remains responsible for compliance with all other requirements of their Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit Mortgages with approved PWRs must be delivered to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 120 days after the Note Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Condominium Unit Mortgage is not delivered within 120 days after the Note Date, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must submit a new PWR and obtain a new Condo Project Advisor Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate prior to delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5702: Planned Unit Developments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (PUDs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5702.1: Requirements for Planned Unit Developments
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Glossary definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Planned Unit Developments (PUDs) and homeowners association
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Units in PUDs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured Homes in PUDs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires the Seller to identify if a unit is located in a PUD. Freddie Mac defines a
elements.label: text

elements.text: PUD as a real estate project in which:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each unit owner holds title to the lot and the improvements on the lot
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The homeowners association holds title to the Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The unit owners have a right to the use of the Common Elements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The unit owners pay a fee to the homeowners association to maintain the Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for their benefit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Zoning is not a basis for classifying a project or subdivision as a PUD. To be classified as
elements.label: text

elements.text: a PUD, units in projects or subdivisions must meet all the requirements above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Glossary definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers should be familiar with the following Glossary terms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary definitions for PUDs
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amenities Amenities are natural features or constructed improvements that enhance
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or project attractiveness and owner or Shareholder enjoyment
elements.label: text

elements.text: but are not essential to the property’s or project’s use by the owners or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shareholders.
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements Common Elements are all portions of a project other than the units or
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual lots within: (1) a Condominium Project which are owned as
elements.label: text

elements.text: tenants in common by the unit owners, (2) a Planned Unit Development
elements.label: text

elements.text: which are owned by the homeowners association and (3) a Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project which are owned by the Cooperative Corporation but not subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a proprietary lease or occupancy agreement. Common Elements are
elements.label: text

elements.text: typically used by unit owners or Shareholders who share in the cost of
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance and operation. Common Elements are defined in the Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document, and may include but are not limited to parking, walkways,
elements.label: text

elements.text: lighting, elevators, boilers, hallways, foyers and legal ingress and egress
elements.label: text

elements.text: to individual units. The term includes common areas and Limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: L
elements.label: text

elements.text: Limited Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Limited Common Elements are portions of Common Elements reserved
elements.label: text

elements.text: for use by one or more unit owners but not all unit owners. Limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements are defined in the Project Documents and may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include, but are not limited to, balconies or patios serving a single unit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigned parking spaces or storage bins.
elements.label: text

elements.text: M
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Association An association that governs (1) multiple affiliated projects, (2) a mixed￾use development, (3) sub-associations within the same condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: project, such as residential and commercial units’ sub-associations, or (4)
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Planned Unit Development (PUD). It typically manages the affairs that
elements.label: text

elements.text: affect the entire planned community or development, whereas the sub￾associations handle the affairs pertaining to their specific project(s) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: areas of the larger development.
elements.label: text

elements.text: P
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project Documents The term Project Documents for a project and any Master Association
elements.label: text

elements.text: refers to any and all documents that pertain to the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Formation of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rights of the developer and limitations on the actions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: developer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgagee rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Operation and governance of the homeowners association
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assessments, voting rights of unit owners and insurance coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Project Documents include but are not limited to the condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: declaration or master deed, by-laws, articles of incorporation (if any),
elements.label: text

elements.text: governing documents, covenants, conditions and restrictions, and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded agreements applicable to the project, as well as any documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Master Association or overall project, and applicable rules and
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations, public offering statement or offering plan, purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement and similar documents. The provisions of the Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents are legally enforceable by the homeowners association.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) PUDs and homeowners association
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reviewing a PUD to determine if it meets the requirement in this section, Sellers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: consider all units and spaces that the homeowners association holds title to or has insured.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In PUDs, the homeowners association owns the Common Elements and maintains them for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the benefit of the unit owners.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Condominium Units in PUDs
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Condominium Unit is located in a PUD, the Seller must comply with the Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements and warranties in Chapter 5701 and the PUD requirements and warranties in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the PUD unit or any PUD Common Element is on a leasehold estate, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the leasehold estate requirements in Chapter 5704 and the PUD requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranties in this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Manufactured Homes in PUDs
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Manufactured Home is located in a PUD, the Seller must comply with the Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home requirements and warranties in Chapter 5703 and the PUD requirements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties in this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must represent and warrant that the property insurance requirements in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4703 have been met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.2: General requirements related to Manufactured
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HUD compliance and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other types of factory-built housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have been built on or after June 15, 1976
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be built on a permanent chassis in compliance with the HUD Codes in effect as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date the Manufactured Home was constructed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be at least 12 feet wide and have at least 400 square feet of above-grade finished area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: This requirement does not apply when the Manufactured Home is an ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (See Section 5601.2(d) for Manufactured Home ADU requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have square footage and room dimensions that are acceptable to typical purchasers in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be permanently affixed to the original permanent foundation, in compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the permanent foundation was installed prior to October 20, 2008, the foundation must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be designed for the site conditions, home design features and loads the home was
elements.label: text

elements.text: designed to withstand in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions or with a design
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a licensed (registered) professional engineer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a foundation that meets all applicable local, State and federal codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an anchoring system that complies with the HUD Codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Manufactured Home was installed prior to October 20, 2008, the anchoring system
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the manufacturer’s design or with a design by a licensed (registered)
elements.label: text

elements.text: professional engineer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Anchoring systems refer to all components of the anchoring and support systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as piers, footings, ties, anchoring equipment, anchoring assemblies and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: equipment, materials and methods of construction that support and secure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home to the ground.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not have wheels, axles or towing hitches
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be permanently connected to utilities (e.g., power, water, sewage disposal system) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the HUD Codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be zoned for residential use and not for commercial or business uses; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Must conform to all applicable use restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any improvements, modifications or repairs that affect the safety, soundness or structural
elements.label: text

elements.text: integrity of the Manufactured Home must be completed prior to the sale of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: This requirement does not apply when the Mortgage is secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home that is a CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgage or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgage (pursuant to the terms defined in Section 4607.1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any structural modifications to an Existing Manufactured Home must be verified as
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by a licensed professional engineer or the local, State or federal authority.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If structural modifications or additions to a 1-unit dwelling result in any portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling being classified as a Manufactured Home, the Mortgage secured by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: property must meet the requirements of this Chapter 5703.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not have been moved from its original site and was previously occupied or installed on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanent foundation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) HUD compliance and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To evidence the Manufactured Home is built in compliance with the Federal Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Construction and Safety Standards, the following must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Existing Manufactured Homes: either the HUD Certification Label(s) or HUD Data Plate
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be present and legible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For existing Manufactured Homes, Freddie Mac will accept as alternative documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A “HUD Label Verification Letter,” with the same information contained on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Certification Label(s), from the Institute for Building Technology and Safety
elements.label: text

elements.text: (IBTS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Performance Verification Certificate (PVC) from the IBTS, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the HUD Data Plate from the In-Plant Primary Inspection Agency (IPIA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or manufacturer. (A list of IPIA offices is posted on HUD’s website.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New Manufactured Homes: both the HUD Data Plate and HUD Certification Label(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be present and legible
elements.label: text

elements.text: The HUD Data Plate section of the Manufactured Home Appraisal Report (Form 70B,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home Appraisal Report) must be completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Other types of factory-built housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Freddie Mac definition of Manufactured Homes excludes other types of factory-built
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing that are not subject to the National Manufactured Home Construction and Safety
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standards Act (e.g., modular or panelized housing).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by other types of factory-built housing are not required to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: this Chapter 5703 and may be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the Mortgage otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HUD compliance and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other types of factory-built housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have been built on or after June 15, 1976
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be built on a permanent chassis in compliance with the HUD Codes in effect as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date the Manufactured Home was constructed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be at least 12 feet wide and have at least 400 square feet of above-grade finished area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: This requirement does not apply when the Manufactured Home is an ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (See Section 5601.2(d) for Manufactured Home ADU requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have square footage and room dimensions that are acceptable to typical purchasers in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be permanently affixed to the original permanent foundation, in compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the permanent foundation was installed prior to October 20, 2008, the foundation must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be designed for the site conditions, home design features and loads the home was
elements.label: text

elements.text: designed to withstand in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions or with a design
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a licensed (registered) professional engineer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a foundation that meets all applicable local, State and federal codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an anchoring system that complies with the HUD Codes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Manufactured Home was installed prior to October 20, 2008, the anchoring system
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the manufacturer’s design or with a design by a licensed (registered)
elements.label: text

elements.text: professional engineer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Anchoring systems refer to all components of the anchoring and support systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as piers, footings, ties, anchoring equipment, anchoring assemblies and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: equipment, materials and methods of construction that support and secure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home to the ground.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not have wheels, axles or towing hitches
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be permanently connected to utilities (e.g., power, water, sewage disposal system) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the HUD Codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be zoned for residential use and not for commercial or business uses; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Must conform to all applicable use restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any improvements, modifications or repairs that affect the soundness or structural
elements.label: text

elements.text: integrity of the Manufactured Home must be completed prior to the sale of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: This requirement does not apply when the Mortgage is secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home that is a CHOICERenovation® In Progress Mortgage or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgage (pursuant to the terms defined in Section 4607.1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any structural modifications to an Existing Manufactured Home must be verified as
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by a licensed professional engineer or the local, State or federal authority.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If structural modifications or additions to a 1-unit dwelling result in any portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling being classified as a Manufactured Home, the Mortgage secured by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: property must meet the requirements of this Chapter 5703.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not have been moved from its original site and was previously occupied or installed on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanent foundation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) HUD compliance and documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To evidence the Manufactured Home is built in compliance with the Federal Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Construction and Safety Standards, the following must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Existing Manufactured Homes: either the HUD Certification Label(s) or HUD Data Plate
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be present and legible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For existing Manufactured Homes, Freddie Mac will accept as alternative documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A “HUD Label Verification Letter,” with the same information contained on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Certification Label(s), from the Institute for Building Technology and Safety
elements.label: text

elements.text: (IBTS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Performance Verification Certificate (PVC) from the IBTS, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the HUD Data Plate from the In-Plant Primary Inspection Agency (IPIA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or manufacturer. (A list of IPIA offices is posted on HUD’s website.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New Manufactured Homes: both the HUD Data Plate and HUD Certification Label(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be present and legible
elements.label: text

elements.text: The HUD Data Plate section of the Traditional Appraisal Report must be completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Other types of factory-built housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Freddie Mac definition of Manufactured Homes excludes other types of factory-built
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing that are not subject to the National Manufactured Home Construction and Safety
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standards Act (e.g., modular or panelized housing).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by other types of factory-built housing are not required to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: this Chapter 5703 and may be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the Mortgage otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.3: Property eligibility and occupancy
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements for Mortgages secured by Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Classified as real property under applicable State law, including relevant statutes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations and judicial decisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A 1-unit dwelling comprised of a single section or multiple sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Permanently affixed to a permanent foundation in compliance with HUD codes
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Manufactured Home may be located:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ On an individual lot
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In a Condominium Project; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In a Planned Unit Development (PUD)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Special requirements for a single-wide Manufactured Home in a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage is secured by a single-wide Manufactured Home in a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project, the project eligibility must be determined through a reciprocal review, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 5701.9 must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Land ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: The land on which the Manufactured Home is situated must be owned by the Borrower in
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee simple, except as stated below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes located in a Condominium Project are
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the Condominium Project complies with the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements in either Section 5701.5 for Established Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects or Section 5701.9 for reciprocal project reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes located on leasehold estates that meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements in Chapter 5706 are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac with prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: written approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements for Mortgages secured by a Manufactured Home are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Mortgages secured by a multiwide Manufactured Home, the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Primary Residence; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A second home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Mortgages secured by a single-wide Manufactured Home, the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be a Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.4: Real property, title and lien requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower and Seller affidavit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special requirements by State
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closing instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Real property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, a Mortgage must be secured by a perfected First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: on real estate that consists of the Manufactured Home and the land on which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home is permanently affixed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must take all steps necessary to ensure that the Mortgaged Premises, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home, is real property pursuant to all relevant State laws and regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table outlines the requirements for surrender and cancelation of certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title:
elements.label: text

elements.text: State specific requirements for surrender and cancelation of certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home title
elements.label: text

elements.text: State procedure Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: States with surrender
elements.label: text

elements.text: and cancelation
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a State has laws, regulations or administrative policies that
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish procedures allowing for the surrender and
elements.label: text

elements.text: cancelation of the certificate of title, the Seller must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with these procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: The Borrower may have to file evidence of intent
elements.label: text

elements.text: or affidavit of affixture for a Manufactured Home to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: converted to real property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: States without surrender
elements.label: text

elements.text: and cancelation
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a State does not have procedures allowing for the surrender
elements.label: text

elements.text: and cancelation of the certificate of title, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with requirements for evidencing and perfecting a lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Manufactured Home on the certificate of title.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A State may require a foundation form to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded in the land records and filed with the agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: governing manufactured housing to complete the process for
elements.label: text

elements.text: converting it to real property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: States with no formal
elements.label: text

elements.text: process for surrendering
elements.label: text

elements.text: and cancelation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage secured by a real estate lien on a Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home evidenced and perfected using a certificate of title is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac only if a State has no
elements.label: text

elements.text: statutory, regulatory or formal administrative process for
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrendering and canceling a certificate of title or exemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a Manufactured Home from the certificate of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The taxation of the Mortgaged Premises as real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: is not evidence that all the appropriate steps to convert a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home to real property have been completed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: that a Manufactured Home is legally classified as real
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documentation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In all cases, notwithstanding the different processes required by State law, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain in the Mortgage file documentation evidencing that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home is legally classified as real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home is properly titled; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lien on the Manufactured Home and the land on which it is permanently affixed has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been properly created, evidenced and perfected
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documentation must include the serial number(s)/vehicle identification number(s) (VIN)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each section of the Manufactured Home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower and Seller affidavit
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower and, if required, the Seller must sign an affidavit that acknowledges their
elements.label: text

elements.text: intent for the Manufactured Home to be permanently part of the real property that secures the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and that contains any specific language that may be required by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The affidavit must be recorded, if permitted, and either a copy of the affidavit showing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: record location or the original after its return from recordation must be maintained in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Uniform Instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The financing of the Manufactured Home and the land must be evidenced by a First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument that includes the Manufactured Home as part of the real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral and is recorded in the land records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes must be evidenced by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform fixed-rate Note or acceptable ARM Note;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac State-specific Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Manufactured Home must be described in the Security Instrument and the description must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include the year, make, model and serial number(s)/VIN(s) for each section of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home and any other information required by applicable law to identify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be covered under a standard real estate title insurance policy that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complies with Chapter 4702
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identifies the Manufactured Home located on the real property, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insures against any loss if the Manufactured Home is not real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must provide one of the following title policy endorsements for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An American Land Title Association (ALTA) Form 7.1, where available, or ALTA Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7 endorsement; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The endorsement required in the applicable jurisdiction to insure against loss if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home does not constitute real property under State law, such as the T-31
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement in the State of Texas
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The ALTA Form 7 endorsement does not guarantee the Manufactured Home has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: properly converted to real property. If the State has statutory, regulatory or formal
elements.label: text

elements.text: administrative process for surrendering and canceling a certificate of title, the Seller is
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for ensuring that all steps necessary to convert the Manufactured Home to real
elements.label: text

elements.text: property have been completed and documentation evidencing the conversion is maintained in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Special requirements by State
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Non-certificate of title States
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table indicates the requirements for classification of real property and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation needed for a property in a non-certificate of title State:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-certificate of title States
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Manufactured Home is classified as real property when it meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements imposed by the State, including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the permanent attachment of the Manufactured Home to the land. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: State does not require that the owner of the Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain a certificate of title for the Manufactured Home (“non￾certificate of title States”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Title to the Manufactured Home is evidenced and conveyed by deed,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the lien on the Manufactured Home and the land is created and
elements.label: text

elements.text: perfected by a recorded Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For non-certificate of title States, the Seller must maintain in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information stating the legal basis (statutory or common law) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining that the Manufactured Home is real property that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: exempt from certificate of title requirements (this information
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be included in the documentation from the title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: company); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence that a certificate of title has not been issued, such as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: manufacturer’s statement of origin (provided that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: manufacturer’s statement of origin is not required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrendered to a State agency)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Certificate of title surrender States
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table indicates the requirements for classification of real property and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation needed for a property in a certificate of title surrender State:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate of title surrender States
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a certificate of title surrender State, the Manufactured Home is
elements.label: text

elements.text: classified as real property when it meets the requirements imposed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the State, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home is permanently affixed to the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The certificate of title is surrendered to the appropriate State
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The certificate of title is canceled by the State agency (“certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title surrender States”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Some certificate of title surrender States also provide for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: exemption from the issuance of a certificate of title upon the
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrender of the manufacturer’s statement of origin if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home is permanently affixed to the land and meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: other requirements for a surrender and cancelation of a certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the certificate of title has been canceled or otherwise exempted,
elements.label: text

elements.text: title to the Manufactured Home is evidenced and conveyed by deed,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the lien on the Manufactured Home and the land is created and
elements.label: text

elements.text: perfected by a recorded Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certificate of title surrender States, the Seller must maintain in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file documentation from the appropriate State authority to
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence that the certificate of title has been surrendered and canceled
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or, in the case of a New Manufactured Home, the manufacturer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: statement of origin has been surrendered and no certificate of title was
elements.label: text

elements.text: issued).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must maintain the following in the Mortgage file as
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A certificate of cancelation, notification letter or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: acknowledgement from the State motor vehicle administration or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other State agency to which the certificate of title was
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrendered; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the documents submitted in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: surrender, along with evidence that the documents were delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: and received by the appropriate State agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: And
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Copies of documents recorded in the land records as part of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: title surrender procedures to show the Manufactured Home has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been converted to real property, such as an affidavit of affixture
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Certificate of title States
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table indicates the requirements for classification of real property and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation needed for a property in a certificate of title State:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate of title States
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a certificate of title State, the State does not have procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowing for the surrender and cancelation of the certificate of title.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Manufactured Home is recognized as part of the real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: when it meets the requirements imposed by the State, including, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home is permanently affixed to the land, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Manufactured Home meets all other conditions to be legally
elements.label: text

elements.text: recognized as real property when a certificate of title has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: issued by the State but not surrendered (“certificate of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: States”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a certificate of title State:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ownership of the Manufactured Home is shown on the certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title, and ownership of the land is shown on the recorded deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of ownership of the Manufactured Home is either noted
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the certificate of title or by the issuance of a new certificate of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title showing the purchaser as the owner, and transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership of the land is done through a recorded deed of transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lien on the land is created by the Security Instrument and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidenced and perfected by recording the Security Instrument in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the land records. The lien on the Manufactured Home is created
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the same Security Instrument but is evidenced and perfected by
elements.label: text

elements.text: its notation on the certificate of title to the Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or filing with the appropriate State agency or another
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified method.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certificate of title States, the Seller must maintain the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original certificate of title showing all intervening
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments and ending with the Seller and its successors in
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests and assigns as lienholder if State law does not require the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original certificate of title to be held by the Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: owner, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the certificate of title showing all intervening
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignments and ending showing the Seller and its successors in
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests and assigns as lienholder if State law requires the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original certificate of title to be held by the Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: owner
elements.label: text

elements.text: And
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence on the certificate of title (original or copy, as applicable)
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the assignment of the security interest in the Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home from the Transferor Servicer to the Transferee Servicer if
elements.label: text

elements.text: there has been a Transfer of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes in certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title States are not eligible for registration with the MERS®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Special
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certificate of title State, the Seller must comply with all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In addition to the requirements for perfecting a lien on the land to

elements.text: contribution is determined as follows:

elements.label: text

elements.text: If, as of the Application Received
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date…
elements.label: text

elements.text: Then…

elements.label: text

elements.text: 12 months or more…
elements.label: text

elements.text: The equity contribution is the current appraised
elements.label: text

elements.text: value of the land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower has owned the land for
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than 12 months….
elements.label: text

elements.text: The equity contribution is the lower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current appraised value of the land; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purchase price of the land

elements.label: text

elements.text: proof of title transfer and satisfaction of any existing liens on the traded Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Layering of risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Manufactured Home adds a layer of collateral risk that must be considered when
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating the overall risk of the Mortgage using the three Cs of underwriting (credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation, capacity and collateral).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must consider this high-risk characteristic in evaluating the overall risk of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and avoid combining a Manufactured Home with weaknesses in the components of
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity and credit reputation. See Section 5102.2 for more information on evaluating
elements.label: text

elements.text: layering of risk and how to document that the overall risk of the Mortgage is acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example 1: If the Borrower has a strong credit reputation and strong capacity to offset the
elements.label: text

elements.text: high risk within the collateral component, a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home with
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum financing would be acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example 2: If the Borrower has weaknesses in credit reputation, such as a short credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: history or derogatory credit information, the layering of risk across credit reputation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral is excessive and would make the Mortgage unacceptable.
