elements.text: 5703.9: Appraisal requirements for Manufactured
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain an appraisal with an interior and exterior inspection that meets Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac requirements. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraiser qualifications for appraisals of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information provided to appraiser for New Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal requirements for a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales comparison approach for Manufactured Homes (excluding CHOICEHomes®)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cost approach for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income approach for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completion documentation for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Appraiser qualifications for appraisals of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the appraiser qualifications stated in Section 5603.1, the Seller must determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the appraiser demonstrates the knowledge and experience to perform quality appraisals
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Manufactured Homes. The Seller must ensure the appraiser:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has adequate experience and previously completed real property appraisals of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has adequate education and/or training related to the appraisal of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Understands the unique features that affect the quality of Manufactured Homes and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: factory construction techniques for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Understands the manufacturers’ and federal, State and local requirements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: installation of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has knowledge concerning the local Manufactured Home market; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has access to appropriate data sources to establish an opinion of value
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The N.A.D.A. Manufactured Housing Appraisal Guide® and Marshall & Swift®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential Cost Handbook may also be used to provide support for the appraiser’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality adjustments and value conclusions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Information provided to appraiser for New Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements in Section 5603.3, for a New Manufactured Home, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must provide the appraiser with:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Contracts: A complete copy of the executed contract for sale of the Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the land is required. If the Manufactured Home and land have separate contracts, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed contract for each is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has owned the land for 12 months or more as of the Application Received
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, a copy of the executed contract for the land is not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Manufacturer documents: A copy of the manufacturer’s invoice and the Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Purchase Agreement is required for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction Mortgage secured by a New Manufactured Home; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Construction to Permanent Mortgage when either a purchase transaction Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Plans and specifications: If the Manufactured Home is not yet affixed to the land, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal may be based on plans and specifications or an existing model Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Appraisal requirements for a Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the property eligibility and valuation requirements of Topic 5600, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A match of the manufacturer’s serial number(s) and the HUD Certification Label
elements.label: text

elements.text: number(s) on the dwelling with the number(s) on the contract for sale, manufacturer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: invoice and any other documentation provided.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the numbers do not match, the appraisal report must state that the Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home is not the same dwelling referenced on the contract for sale or other applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A photo(s) of the HUD Data Plate and HUD Certification Label(s) as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an existing Manufactured Home, photo(s) of either the HUD Data Plate or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HUD Certification Label(s) are acceptable. When both are present, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must include both.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a New Manufactured Home, photos of both the HUD Data Plate and HUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certification Label(s) are required
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A sales comparison approach to support an opinion of the market value of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home that excludes non-realty items, such as, but not limited to, insurance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties or furniture
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. A cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Analyses, opinions and conclusions reported on Form 70B, Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Sales comparison approach for Manufactured Homes (excluding CHOICEHomes)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome, when completing the sales comparison
elements.label: text

elements.text: approach for Manufactured Homes, the following must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must identify the specific number of Manufactured Home sales and
elements.label: text

elements.text: listings as well as the respective price ranges that were used in the analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must include at least two comparable Manufactured Home sales of
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar configuration (i.e., single-wide comparable sales for a single-wide subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property and multiwide comparable sales for a multiwide subject property) and similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report may contain either site-built housing or a different type of factory￾built housing as the third comparable sale if an explanation is provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report may contain more than three comparable sales, including contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales and listings, to support the appraiser’s opinion of value
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Manufactured Home is in a controlled market (e.g., a new subdivision or project, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: newly converted project or an area where the property seller owns a substantial number of
elements.label: text

elements.text: units), at least one comparable sale must be outside the influence of the developer, builder or
elements.label: text

elements.text: property seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Resales from within the subject project or subdivision may be used to meet this
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When comparable sales from outside the subject project or subdivision are used, they
elements.label: text

elements.text: must also be outside the influence of the subject property’s developer, builder or property
elements.label: text

elements.text: seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must not contain comparable sales that were created by combining
elements.label: text

elements.text: vacant land sales with the contract purchase price of the Manufactured Home. If the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report does not contain at least two comparable sales of similar Manufactured Homes, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5703.12(h) for details regarding appraisal requirements for Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by a CHOICEHome.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Cost approach for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: A detailed cost approach based on published sources and supported by market data is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required for all Manufactured Home appraisals. The appraisal report must provide sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and data to allow the Seller or other reviewers to replicate the cost figures and
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser should fully develop the cost approach to support quality of construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: determinations and any adjustments made by the appraiser to account for differences in
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality of construction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Key criteria include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The cost figures must come directly from a published cost service without modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must provide the source and the effective date of the cost data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must contain the cost data with a quality rating that is based only on
elements.label: text

elements.text: objective criteria.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The appraisal report may contain other sources of objective information, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Uniform Appraisal Dataset Specifications (as found in Exhibit 36, Condition and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Quality Ratings and Level of Updating Definitions) ratings and definitions, to support the
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality rating.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must contain an opinion of site value supported by a summary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable land sales or other methods used for estimating site value
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Income approach for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income approach is not required; however, it must be provided if necessary to develop a
elements.label: text

elements.text: credible appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Completion documentation for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the appraisal is performed before the Manufactured Home is delivered and installed on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanent foundation, the Seller must document that the Manufactured Home is complete.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a new appraisal is required, the appraisal report must contain an analysis of previously
elements.label: text

elements.text: unavailable information. If the new appraisal is an appraisal update that also acts as
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation of completion, it must identify that the conditions of the underlying appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: have been satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: information and data to allow the Seller or other reviewers to replicate the cost figures and
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser should fully develop the cost approach to support quality of construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: determinations and any adjustments made by the appraiser to account for differences in
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality of construction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Key criteria include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The cost figures must come directly from a published cost service without modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must provide the source and the effective date of the cost data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must contain the cost data with a quality rating that is based only on
elements.label: text

elements.text: objective criteria.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The appraisal report may use Appendix 2: Condition and Quality Ratings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions contained in Appendix F-1: URAR Reference Guide to support the quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: rating.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraisal report must contain an opinion of site value supported by a summary of
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable land sales or other methods used for estimating site value
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Income approach for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income approach is not required; however, it must be provided if necessary to develop a
elements.label: text

elements.text: credible appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Completion documentation for Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the appraisal is performed before the Manufactured Home is delivered and installed on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanent foundation, the Seller must document that the Manufactured Home is complete.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a new appraisal is required, the appraisal report must contain an analysis of previously
elements.label: text

elements.text: unavailable information. If the new appraisal is an appraisal update that also acts as
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation of completion, it must identify that the conditions of the underlying appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: have been satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5703.10: Mortgage insurance requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home must have mortgage insurance coverage as stated
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 4701.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage can include Borrower-paid mortgage insurance premiums as provided for in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4701.2.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: State law or the local taxing authority may require the Mortgaged Premises to be taxed as either
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate or personal property by the jurisdiction where it is located. The following table
elements.label: text

elements.text: outlines how to determine property taxation:

elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, all of the requirements relating to real estate taxes in the Guide apply equally to

elements.text: personal property taxes applicable to a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home.

elements.text: 5703.12: Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEHome® is a type of Manufactured Home with aesthetics, architectural elements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling specifications that exceed the minimum standards required for HUD-coded
elements.label: text

elements.text: manufactured homes. These factory-built properties have features comparable to conventional,
elements.label: text

elements.text: site-built properties. This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase of Mortgages secured by CHOICEHomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Construction elements for CHOICEHomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan purpose and occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible and ineligible Mortgages secured by CHOICEHomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal requirements for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underwriting requirements for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: TLTV (HTLTV) ratios and value calculation for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of proceeds for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage insurance for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Purchase of Mortgages secured by CHOICEHomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must verify that the subject property meets CHOICEHome property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. This includes obtaining an appraisal report and, when required, a final
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection that evidences the presence of the CHOICEHome element requirements and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Freddie Mac CHOICEHome notice or the Fannie Mae MH Advantage® Sticker,
elements.label: text

elements.text: labels that certify the Manufactured Home meets certain CHOICEHome or MH
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advantage construction elements, located in proximity to the HUD Data Plate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a new CHOICEHome, both the HUD Data Plate and the HUD Certification Label(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an existing CHOICEHome, the HUD Data Plate or the HUD Certification Label(s). If
elements.label: text

elements.text: neither the HUD Data Plate nor the HUD Certification Label(s) are present or legible,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will accept the alternative documentation described in Section 5703.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The presence of a CHOICEHome notice only certifies a dwelling was manufactured to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEHome specifications that were in place as of its manufacture date and does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: guarantee eligibility as a CHOICEHome.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Construction elements for CHOICEHomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEHomes must meet the Manufactured Home general requirements in Section 5703.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: and property and occupancy requirements in Section 5703.3, except as otherwise indicated
elements.label: text

elements.text: by specific CHOICEHome construction elements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Foundation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The permanent foundation must comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Meet HUD’s Permanent Foundation Guide for Manufactured Housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an engineered foundation certified by a registered architect or professional
elements.label: text

elements.text: engineer; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a masonry or poured concrete perimeter wall
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Elevation design and features
elements.label: text

elements.text: The elevation design and features must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a low-profile/residential set finished floor set
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not exceed 30 inches from the bottom of the first-floor joist to the exterior grade for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the front or entry
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a covered porch (minimum 72 sq. ft.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)New construction features and site improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A newly constructed CHOICEHome with a date of manufacture after June 4, 2026, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have all of the following features and site improvements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a single-section home, an attached garage or carport with space to accommodate
elements.label: text

elements.text: at least one car. The exterior materials and finishes for the attached garage must be at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least equivalent to the remainder of the structure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A driveway leading to the home or to the garage or carport. The driveway must
elements.label: text

elements.text: consist of blacktop, pavers, bricks, concrete or cement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A sidewalk connecting the driveway to the front entrance of the home. The sidewalk
elements.label: text

elements.text: must consist of blacktop, pavers, flagstone, bricks, concrete or cement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: If an existing in-fill lot cannot accommodate an attached garage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: carport, driveway and/or sidewalk, the feature and/or site improvement is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required. An in-fill lot is vacant or underutilized land located within a developed
elements.label: text

elements.text: urban or suburban area, typically surrounded by existing buildings and infrastructure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: It specifically excludes any lot located in a newly created or recently platted
elements.label: text

elements.text: subdivision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A gutter and downspout system to direct water away from the foundation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Loan purpose and occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage secured by a CHOICEHome must be either a purchase transaction Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage secured by a 1-unit Primary Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Eligible and ineligible Mortgages secured by CHOICEHomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fixed-rate Mortgages; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 5/6-Month, 7/6-Month or 10/6-Month ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible® Mortgages, HomeOne® Mortgages and HeritageOne® Mortgages must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be fixed-rate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome must not be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Super conforming Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Appraisal requirements for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Mortgage secured by a CHOICEHome requires an appraisal report completed on Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 70B, Manufactured Home Appraisal Report. The appraisal report must reflect an opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: value based on the real property as completed, consisting of the CHOICEHome, all site
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements and the land on which the property is situated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include photos of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The CHOICEHome notice or MH Advantage Sticker, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The HUD Data Plate and HUD Certification Label(s) as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For a new CHOICEHome, both the HUD Data Plate and the HUD Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Label(s) must be present and legible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For an existing CHOICEHome, the HUD Data Plate or the HUD Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Label(s) are acceptable. When both are present, the appraiser must include both.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property and site features, including porches, garages, carports and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: attached or detached structures
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must comply with the sales comparison approach requirements outlined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 5605.6, and the appraisal report should contain at least one comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEHome sale. If none are available, the appraisal report may contain site-built homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: as comparable sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Traditional HUD-coded Manufactured Homes are not required as comparable sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if used, the appraisal report must include an explanation that supports its use as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sale(s) considering CHOICEHome’s elements and specifications.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a low-profile/residential set finished floor set
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not exceed 30 inches from the bottom of the first-floor joist to the exterior grade for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the front or entry
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have a covered porch (minimum 72 sq. ft.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)New construction features and site improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A newly constructed CHOICEHome with a date of manufacture after June 4, 2026, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have all of the following features and site improvements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a single-section home, an attached garage or carport with space to accommodate
elements.label: text

elements.text: at least one car. The exterior materials and finishes for the attached garage must be at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least equivalent to the remainder of the structure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A driveway leading to the home or to the garage or carport. The driveway must
elements.label: text

elements.text: consist of blacktop, pavers, bricks, concrete or cement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A sidewalk connecting the driveway to the front entrance of the home. The sidewalk
elements.label: text

elements.text: must consist of blacktop, pavers, flagstone, bricks, concrete or cement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: If an existing in-fill lot cannot accommodate an attached garage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: carport, driveway and/or sidewalk, the feature and/or site improvement is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required. An in-fill lot is vacant or underutilized land located within a developed
elements.label: text

elements.text: urban or suburban area, typically surrounded by existing buildings and infrastructure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: It specifically excludes any lot located in a newly created or recently platted
elements.label: text

elements.text: subdivision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A gutter and downspout system to direct water away from the foundation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Loan purpose and occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage secured by a CHOICEHome must be either a purchase transaction Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage secured by a 1-unit Primary Residence.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fixed-rate Mortgages; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 5/6-Month, 7/6-Month or 10/6-Month ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home Possible® Mortgages, HomeOne® Mortgages and HeritageOne® Mortgages must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be fixed-rate Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Ineligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome must not be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Converted Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller-Owned Modified Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Super conforming Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Appraisal requirements for Mortgages secured by a CHOICEHome
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Mortgage secured by a CHOICEHome requires a Traditional Appraisal Report. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must identify the subject property as a Manufactured Home and include an
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinion of value based on the real property as completed, consisting of the CHOICEHome,
elements.label: text

elements.text: all site improvements and the land on which the property is situated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include photos of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The CHOICEHome notice or MH Advantage Sticker, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The HUD Data Plate and HUD Certification Label(s) as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For a new CHOICEHome, both the HUD Data Plate and the HUD Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Label(s) must be present and legible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For an existing CHOICEHome, the HUD Data Plate or the HUD Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Label(s) are acceptable. When both are present, the appraisal report must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: both.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subject property and site features, including porches, garages, carports and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: attached or detached structures
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must be in compliance with the sales comparison approach requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: outlined in Section 5605.7, and the appraisal report should contain at least one comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: CHOICEHome sale. If none are available, the appraisal report may contain site-built homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: as comparable sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Traditional HUD-coded Manufactured Homes are not required as comparable sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if used, the appraisal report must include an explanation that supports its use as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sale(s) considering CHOICEHome’s elements and specifications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5704: Leasehold Estates
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5704.1: Purchase of leasehold Mortgages (06/04/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estate eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lease provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Projects on leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase a Mortgage secured by either a leasehold estate or a leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a ground lease community where there is a demonstrated market acceptance of this type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: property ownership. The Mortgage must be secured by a leasehold interest in the land or ground
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease and the property improvements to be a leasehold Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following property types are eligible to secure leasehold Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Planned Unit Development (PUD) units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to the following Guide provisions for details on:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other Guide provisions related to leasehold mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Community Land Trust Mortgage requirements Chapter 4502
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes located on leasehold estates Chapter 5706
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne® Mortgages, including the special requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: HeritageOne Mortgages that are leasehold Mortgages in Section 4504.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4504
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: As used in this chapter, the following terms have the meanings ascribed to them below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase of leasehold Mortgage terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Basic rent The amount paid for the use of the leasehold estate under the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lease (or sublease, if applicable). Basic rent does not include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Utilities for the leasehold estate or common areas, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Use fees or operating expenses for the common areas, facilities and
elements.label: text

elements.text: services
elements.label: text

elements.text: G
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ground lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: community
elements.label: text

elements.text: A planned residential development, including infrastructure, common
elements.label: text

elements.text: areas and community facilities for use by the individual lessee, with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following characteristics:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ Under the terms of the lease, the individual lessee holds a real
elements.label: text

elements.text: property leasehold estate in a parcel of land improved by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling and has an undivided common interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure, common areas and community facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ◼ The ground lease community is either a PUD or Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project, administered by a homeowners association; or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: community is administered by the fee simple land owner/lessor
elements.label: text

elements.text: that owns, and is obligated under the lease to maintain, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure, common areas and community facilities for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: common use and benefit of the individual lessees
elements.label: text

elements.text: L
elements.label: text

elements.text: Leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee
elements.label: text

elements.text: The mortgagee that has a lien on the lessee’s (or sublessee’s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold estate, including improvements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Leasehold estate eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must ensure that the following eligibility requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The leasehold estate and property improvement must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Constitute real property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be covered by a title insurance policy that complies with the applicable requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Chapter 4702
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease (and any sublease including all amendments) must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Recorded in the appropriate land records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ In full force and effect, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Binding and enforceable against the lessor (and sublessor)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The leasehold estate and Mortgage must not be impaired by any merger of the fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest and leasehold interest in the event the same person or entity acquires both
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The term of the leasehold estate must run for at least five years beyond the maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage unless the fee simple title vests at an earlier date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All basic rent and amounts due (for taxes, insurance, utilities and use fees or operating
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses) relating to the land and improvements must be current, and the Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be in default under any provision of the lease, nor may the lessor have claimed such
elements.label: text

elements.text: a default
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease must not preclude the Borrower from retaining voting rights in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeowners association, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All applicable Servicing requirements under the Guide and other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be met
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Lease provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must ensure that the following lease requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease must permit assignments, transfers, mortgaging and subletting of the leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or subleasehold) estate, including any improvements on the leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease must provide that for a notice of lessee’s default (monetary or non-monetary) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be valid, the lessor must send written notice of the lessee’s default to the leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee not more than 30 days after such default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The lease is not required to include a notice of default provision if the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is located in Maryland and applicable State law provides for all lessors to
elements.label: text

elements.text: register residential leases with the State and requires the lessor to send written notice of
elements.label: text

elements.text: default to the leasehold mortgagee no less than 30 days prior to the lessor filing an action
elements.label: text

elements.text: of possession.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease must provide for the right of the leasehold mortgagee, in its sole discretion, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: cure a default for the lessee’s (or sublessee, if applicable) account within the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted to lessee or take over the rights under the lease (sublease)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease cannot contain default provisions allowing forfeiture or termination of the lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: for non-monetary default
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease must provide for protection of the mortgagee’s interests, including an insurable
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in the subject property (unless otherwise required by law) and interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease, ground lease community and leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lease may, but is not required to, include an option for the Borrower to purchase the
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee interest; however, there can be no time limit on when the option must be exercised,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the lease and option to purchase must be assignable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Projects on leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase a Mortgage secured by a unit in a Condominium Project (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.2(b)(3)) or PUD (see Section 5702.1(c)) situated on a leasehold estate if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in this section are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5704.2: Appraisal requirements for leasehold
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (01/26/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the other Guide requirements related to appraisals, an appraisal for each leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage must meet the requirements in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For special appraisal requirements for HeritageOne® Mortgages that are leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, see Section 4504.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For leasehold Mortgages, the appraiser must develop a detailed description of the terms,
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions and restrictions of the ground lease. The appraiser must consider and report any effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the lease have on the value and marketability of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When there are similar leasehold sales available that have the same lease terms, the appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: should use these sales as comparable sales. If sales of properties with the same lease terms are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not available, the appraiser should use other similar leasehold sales having different lease terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: as comparable sales. The appraiser must describe the differences in the terms of the leases and
elements.label: text

elements.text: report any effect the differences have on the value and marketability of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are no sales of leasehold properties, the appraiser should use sales of similar properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned in fee simple as comparable sales. The appraiser must explain why the use of sales with
elements.label: text

elements.text: different property rights is appropriate and make appropriate adjustments to reflect the market’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: reaction to these differences.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Condominium Projects and Planned Unit Developments (PUDs) on leasehold estates, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser must also:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide a description of the Common Elements, including Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comment on the ground rent for the subject property and the competing properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5704.2: Appraisal requirements for leasehold
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the other Guide requirements related to appraisals, an appraisal report for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold Mortgage must meet the requirements in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For special appraisal requirements for HeritageOne® Mortgages that are leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, see Section 4504.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For leasehold Mortgages, the appraisal report must include the ground rent and the conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: and restrictions of the ground lease. The appraisal report must report the effect the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lease have on the value and marketability of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When there are similar leasehold sales available that have the same lease terms, these sales

elements.text: (a) Seller’s assessment of Cooperative Project risks
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires a Cooperative Project review to address certain project risks, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to:

elements.text: ■ The marketability and condition of the project
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The financial stability and viability of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project-level litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Restrictions on Shareholders’ rights to occupy the unit; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The adequacy of insurance coverage to protect the project from damage and loss
elements.label: text
